title: lauraceae
intro: The Lauraceae rank, also known as the Laurel family, is a taxonomic group of flowering plants that belong to a specific classification level. It is a family of plants that comprises approximately 2,000 species, including the well-known laurel and avocado trees. The plants within the Lauraceae family are typically evergreen, aromatic, and have simple leaves, with many species found in tropical and subtropical regions around the world. The diversity of the Lauraceae family is characterized by a wide range of species, from shrubs to large trees, with many species used for their timber, fruit, and medicinal properties.
